Roof plan — draw your sections Click corners to draw each section, then label it
🏠
Step 1: Draw building outline
Click corners clockwise around your building. Press Enter or click Finish to complete.
⚡ Auto-generate roof lines from outline
Use this to flip the ridge direction on gable/dutch, or the slope direction on mono-pitch.
💡 Hold Shift while dragging a corner to disable the rectangle-stretch (lets you make diagonal / non-square roofs)  |  Right-click any line to delete
Roof lines
Details
Zoom: 100%
Rotate: 0.0°
💡 Best workflow: Upload photo → click Building outline → click each corner of the roof perimeter clockwise → press Enter to close → then draw internal lines (ridge, valley etc) on top.

Labour & scaffolding Set the hours; cost/hr is what you pay, price/hr is the charge-out rate
Material Price Quantities auto-calc from your drawing — adjust any row manually

Flashing quantities include a 1.2× wastage allowance. Drop-down on each row lets you swap product variants (add more in Settings → Price book).

Draw your roof on the Map Roof tab and set unit prices in Settings → Price book — this table will auto-populate.

Job profitability Revenue (charge-out) vs cost — refresh material price above to update
From your drawing measurements

Draw lines and add measurements on the Map Roof tab, then click Calibrate scale to populate this automatically.

Roof sheet plan — colour-coded cut layout

Draw your roof outline + gutter/hip/valley lines with measurements, then refresh to see the sheet cut plan.

Materials — pick your profile / grade / thickness; cut lists update live from the drawing

Draw your roof + add line measurements on the Map Roof tab, then refresh — cut lists for sheets, ridge and each flashing type will appear here.

Consumables — paper / screws / rivets / dektites

Quantities calculated from roof area + total flashing run.

Job & delivery — prints on the order PDF + supplier email

Flood Roofing Ltd | Whangarei, Northland, NZ | floodroofing.co.nz
Quote
Ref: FR-001  ·  Total incl. GST: $0.00
Quote settings & extras — the quote text (client, scope, conditions, notes, terms, price) is edited directly on the proposal above. These are optional settings & photos.
Quote inclusions
Optional sections to append to the printed/emailed quote.
Edit inspection report content →
The inspection report fields (condition, photos, scope notes, custom sections) are populated from the Map Roof and Scope & labour tabs. The report preview is rendered below in the legacy #tab-report container — it stays hidden in the app, but prints when this option is on.
Quote details
Line items

Default off — only the combined Total / GST / Deposit prints on the Price page. Tick to also itemise the line items below the total.

Description Qty Unit ($) Line total
Scope, notes & terms
Existing roof condition— shown on the inspection-report page

Roof type & pitch default to your Map Roof drawing if left blank.

Guttering options— prices for the gutter-type dropdown

Enter the add-on price (ex-GST) for each gutter type. The customer picks one from a dropdown on the proposal's Gutters page and the total updates live. Leave at 0 to show the option without a price.

Totals
Total incl. GST
$0.00
Subtotal$0.00
GST$0.00
Proposal options — give the client 2–3 packages to pick from
Each option becomes its own page in the printed proposal. The client tick-selects which option(s) they want — the summary page rolls up the selected total in real time.
Proposal sections— pages, cover title & about us
Tick which sections to include in the printed/emailed proposal.
Stock proposal photos — past Colorsteel jobs, hero shots, etc. Saved across all proposals.
These appear on the cover, About, and Colorsteel Maxam pages of every proposal — upload a handful of your best Colorsteel re-roof photos once and they're set. Site-specific photos (uploaded under the inspection) appear on the Project details page only.
Proposal image slots — assign a photo to each fixed-ratio slot in the proposal.
Each slot has a locked aspect ratio so the proposal layout stays consistent. Click Choose to pick from Stock photos, this job's Photos tab, or Upload directly. Photos are auto-cropped centred to the slot. Leave a slot unset to fall back to the stock library in order.
Proposal — click any text to edit
Order material
Send to: — pick a supplier or type one —
Job & delivery
Picked up from / delivered to Flood Roofing yard — supplier picks the standard yard address from your supplier record.
Roof specification
5. Cut list

Sheet count + length combos. Sync pulls the latest from the Materials & Sheet Layout tab; edit freely after — your changes are kept.

DescriptionQtyLength (mm)
Empty — click Sync from sheet layout or + Add row.
11. Flashings

Each flashing: pick a type, enter the length of every face (a face = one bend section). "Line breaks" records where a continuous run joins — e.g. "join at 6.0m". Use Sketch for a quick custom profile.

13. Extras / notes for supplier

Sketch custom flashing — Flashing #1
Girth: 0 mm  ·  Angle changes: 0 Click to place corners · Finish or press Enter when done · Add measurement to label segments in mm · Painted side places a colour-arrow.
Click to place corners, again for the next corner. Press Finish (or Enter) when done. Saves as a re-usable library entry.
Saved flashings library
Saves are stored on your account. Use Save to library on any flashing card to add to this list — saved defaults pre-fill below so you only edit qty + measurements before adding.

Coming soon. The adapter is on the roadmap. For now, save your API key in Settings → Integrations and use Fergus or Email Quote.

⚙ Configure connection
Connection status
Checking proxy...
API connection issue.
Proxy running. Connecting to Fergus...
Fergus API key

The Fergus PAT used for connections is read from FERGUS_API_KEY on the backend (Railway env var). The field below is informational only — changes here are not sent to Fergus.

Connected to Fergus successfully.
No JMS picked. Choose one from the dropdown to configure its connection.
Autosave

When autosave is on, every change you make to job details, the roof drawing, scope, materials, and quote fields is saved against the currently-open job. Reopening the job restores everything.

Autosave only fires while a job is open (the chip in the sidebar shows the job number).

Link Job Management System

Pick the platform you run jobs in. Selecting a system opens its setup page where you enter API keys and test the connection. You can still push to a different system per-quote via the Push to JMS button on the Quote tab.

No JMS linked. Quotes will print/email locally only.
Settings
Selectable quote options
Toggle which optional line items appear by default on new quotes (gutter clean, scaffold, asbestos disposal, etc.)
Custom report sections
Extra sections appended to the inspection report (e.g. health & safety statement, council compliance notes).

Unit prices used to auto-cost the Materials & Sheet Layout tab. The combined total becomes the Materials line on every Quote.

📤 Upload supplier price book CSV (immediate) or PDF (AI parse)

CSV columns: description, unit, price (header row optional). Parsed rows go into Custom price-book items below.

Roofing sheets — add a row per product variant
The Materials tab cost uses this product's $/lm × total gutter length (sheets are ordered by gutter run × number of sheets across).
Underlay / paper — $ per roll, by size
$
$
$
Flashings & fixings — default unit prices
Custom price-book items — special items beyond the auto-quantified list (skylights, dektites, vents, etc.)

Default hour budgets used by the editable Labour breakdown on the Scope & Pricing tab. The user can still tweak hours per job; this just sets the starting numbers.

Default hours per scope item — pre-multiplier; the Scope & Pricing labour table multiplies by complexity

Manage the lists that drive the dropdowns on the Order Material tab. Click Save settings at the top of this card to persist changes to your account.

Suppliers — companies you buy material from
Roof products — e.g. Colorsteel Maxam, Colorcote, Zincalume
Underlays — e.g. Thermakraft 215, Bitumen building paper
Screws — e.g. 12g × 65mm Tek hex, 14g × 100mm timber screw
Flashing types — show as options in the Flashings dropdown on Order Material

Saved flashing drawings — Materials & Sheet Layout shows the matching ones for the selected roof profile. Each saved flashing is one cross-section sketch you can re-use across jobs.

Enter API keys for the job-management systems you use. Keys are stored against your user account and never shared.
Fergus — Material Sales account id
Fergus's partner API doesn't expose your chart of accounts, so paste the numeric id of your "Material Sales" account here. Find it in Fergus → Settings → Sales Account Codes — click the Material Sales row and the id is the trailing number in the URL.
Phase 2: "Send job to JMS" + "Email quote" buttons (and the JMS adapters for ServiceM8, Jobber, Tradify) ship in the next iteration. Fergus push already works from the Fergus tab.
